channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Afghan and Pakistani delegations in Doha for crisis talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghan and Pakistani delegations arrived in the Qatari capital, Doha, on Saturday, hoping to defuse the deadliest crisis between them in several years after more than a week of fighting killed dozens of people and injured hundreds on both sides.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya as thousands turn out to mourn former PM Odinga
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu ahead of the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, after five people died in the last two public funeral events.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As Israelis and Palestinians wait anxiously to see what comes after a ceasefire in the two-year war in Gaza, the experience of the Northern Ireland peace process in the 1990s may offer lessons in the thorny process of moving from seemingly intractable conflict to enduring peace.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: Torrential rains in east-central Mexico last week have left at least 72 dead and raised concerns about the government's ability to alert people to severe weather.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Daily struggles persist in Gaza even as ceasefire offers some respite
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Bassil Naggar can finally sleep without getting jolted awake by the sounds of Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: TOQA EZZIDIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas defends executions of Palestinians after Trump issues stern warning to terror group
item.description: After Trump’s threat, Hamas official Mohammed Nazzal defends the recent executions of Palestinians in Gaza as the group seeks to settle scores with opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Trump's approval shifted after the ceasefire in Gaza, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More U.S. adults support President Donald Trump's handling of the conflict in Israel after he brokered a ceasefire deal in Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds — but his approval ratings on domestic issues remain weak.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: French police arrest 4 in alleged plot targeting exiled Russian activist and Putin critic
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Police in France detained four people suspected in a plot targeting exiled Russian rights activist Vladimir Osechkin, who exposes abuses in Russian prisons, France's national anti-terror prosecution office said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER and EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senators look to block Trump from engaging in ‘hostilities’ in Venezuela
item.description: Senators Tim Kaine and Rand Paul lead bipartisan effort to prevent President Donald Trump from launching military strikes in Venezuela without Congress approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Orbán celebrates Hungary as 'the only place in Europe' where a Trump-Putin meeting can be held
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary's nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán on Friday celebrated his country's status as the host of upcoming talks between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russia's Vladimir Putin, a meeting where the two leaders are expected to discuss an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Trump meets with Ukraine's Zelensky at White House
item.description: President Trump will host Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House on Friday for the third time since returning to office in January. The first visit was a train wreck; the second, which involved European leaders, went more smoothly.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, The Hill

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump's authorization of covert CIA strikes in Venezuela raises concerns of regime change and potential blowback from Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Russia convicts 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges
item.description: A court in southern Russia convicted 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges Friday after a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe's main center-left political group expels Slovak leader's party for breaching its values
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Europe’s main center-left political group on Friday kicked out the party of Slovakian Prime Minister Robert Fico, who is accused of cozying up to Russian President Vladimir Putin and undermining the rule of law in his own country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mongolia's prime minister ousted after 4 months in ruling party feud
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Mongolia ’s parliament has voted to oust the prime minister in an unusually public power struggle within the ruling Mongolian People’s Party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky meets with Energy secretary, US companies ahead of visit with Trump
item.description: Ahead of his upcoming meeting with President Trump on Friday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said he met with Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright and various American energy and defense companies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump is ramping up military pressure on Venezuela’s authoritarian leader Nicolás Maduro, raising the prospect of strikes inside the country, which would mark a major escalation from already controversial military attacks against alleged drug trafficking boats in international waters.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US military drone strike on drug 'submersible' in Caribbean leaves survivors, official confirms
item.description: U.S. military drone strike destroyed a drug-smuggling submersible in the Caribbean Thursday, leaving 2 to 3 survivors in the water, a U.S. official tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN condemns military takeover in Madagascar as coup leader to be quickly sworn in as president
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — The United Nations chief on Thursday condemned the military takeover in Madagascar as an army colonel who led the coup was set to be quickly sworn in as president just three days after announcing soldiers had taken charge. U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French pair imprisoned in Iran over alleged spying have 'reached the limit,' families say
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The families of two French nationals detained in Iran for more than three years said their loved ones have reached “the limit of what they can endure” following a report that an Iranian court sentenced them to decades in prison on spying charges.
item.dc:creator: CATHERINE GASCHKA and THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IDF holds memorial ceremony at base attacked by Hamas on Oct. 7 honoring fallen troops
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) remembered the second anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks on Nahal Oz with a ceremony at the base this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian barrage causes blackouts in Ukraine as Zelenskyy seeks Trump's help
item.description: Russia battered Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in its latest heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida doctor detained by Israel during aid mission to Gaza describes experience
item.description: Two Florida residents who were detained by Israel during an aid mission to Gaza are describing their experience in a Wednesday press conference.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: State Department aware of reports after American tourists attacked, 1 killed in popular seaside destination
item.description: State Department aware of reports from coastal town near Lisbon, Portugal, as Cascais police investigate after two Americans attacked, leaving one dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US blasts China's expansion of rare earth export controls
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Top U.S. officials on Wednesday blasted China's major expansion of rare earth export controls as a threat to global supply chains, but said Beijing could still change course and avoid steps by Washington to decouple from the world's second-largest economy. U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Lawder and Andrea Shalal, NewsNation

item.title: Parents of 2 US citizens demand return of their sons’ bodies and all deceased hostages held by Hamas in Gaza
item.description: Two American citizens, Itay Chen and Omer Neutra, remain among deceased hostages held by Hamas as terror group violates hostage exchange agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia using new, more precise drone tech to attack Ukraine’s vital rail network
item.description: Since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion in early 2022, railway officials have publicly reported about roughly one attack on railways per week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese airlines protest US plan to ban their flights over Russian airspace
item.description: The U.S. side has said such flights give Chinese airlines an unfair cost advantage over American carriers, which cannot cross through Russian airspace.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A new form of tourism: US residents seek tattoos in Mexico
item.description: JUAREZ, Mexico (Border Report) – U.S. residents often cite lower prices and personalized attention when asked why they travel to see doctors and dentists in Mexico. The same holds true for tattoo artists.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Russian aerial attack hits a Ukrainian hospital, days before Zelenskyy meets Trump
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces launched powerful glide bombs and drones against Ukraine’s second-largest city in overnight attacks, hitting a hospital and wounding seven people, an official said Tuesday, as European military aid for Kyiv dropped sharply and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensk
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Trump-brokered peace deal leads to Hamas hostage releases in Israel
item.description: Hamas releases Israeli hostages, including Omri Miran, as twin brothers Ziv and Gali Berman are reunited under President Donald Trump's ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: No end in sight to government shutdown as House speaker refuses to negotiate
item.description: The shutdown entered its 14th day with the Senate returning from its holiday break, Republican House Speaker Mike Johnson refusing to call lawmakers back to Washington and President Donald Trump’s budget director vowing to keep firing federal workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: After hostages and prisoners freed, complex issues remain for Israel-Hamas
item.description: Issues such as whether Hamas will disarm and who will govern Gaza — and the question of Palestinian statehood — remain unresolved, highlighting the fragility of an agreement that for now only pauses the deadliest conflict in the history of Israel and the Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gaza photojournalist says his disabled brother is missing
item.description: A Gaza-based freelance photojournalist said his disabled brother went missing in August after seeking aid. The family hoped he would be returned today.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Living hostages and Palestinian prisoners are released as part of ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — All 20 remaining living hostages held in Gaza and hundreds of Palestinian prisoners held by Israel walked free Monday as part of a ceasefire pausing two years of war that decimated the Gaza Strip and killed tens of thousands of Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump warns Russia he may send Ukraine long-range Tomahawks if Moscow doesn't settle war soon
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump on Sunday warned Russia that he may send Ukraine long-range Tomahawk missiles if Moscow doesn’t settle its war there soon — suggesting that he could be ready to increase the pressure on Vladimir Putin's government using a key weapons system.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9
